name: Sant Marti de Montnegre - circular gravel through Montnegre Park and the Corredor
name_original: Sant Martí de Montnegre - circular gravel por el Parque de Montnegre y el Corredor
region_intro: Touring cycling around Sant Celoni offers diverse landscapes, serving as a gateway to Montseny Natural Park and Montnegre and El Corredor Nature Reserves. The region features dense forests, high-mountain terrain, and a rich hydrological system with numerous springs. Cyclists can navigate varied terrain, from paved roads with progressive ascents to unpaved segments suitable for gravel cycling, including challenging climbs and gentler paths. This area provides a range of cycling experiences, from coastal views to deep forest trails. Best touring cycling routes around Sant Celoni The most popular touring cycling route is Sant Marti de Montnegre - circular gravel through Montnegre Park and the Corredor, an 18.0 miles (28.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 10 minutes to complete. This route offers a challenging gravel experience through the natural reserves. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Llobateres Bird Observatory – Small bird paradise loop from Sant Celoni, a moderate 27.0 miles (43.5 km) path. This route features a relatively flat profile, making it suitable for a more relaxed ride through natural habitats. Local touring cyclists also love the Santa Fe del Montseny – Bordoriol Pass loop from Sant Celoni, a 51.5 miles (82.8 km) trail leading through Montseny Natural Park, often completed in about 6 hours 9 minutes. Touring cycling around Sant Celoni is defined by dense forests, high-mountain landscapes, and varied terrain including both paved and unpaved segments. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult climbs. The routes in Sant Celoni are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 1,100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Sant Celoni's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Sant Celoni?
A: There are over 70 touring cycling routes available around Sant Celoni, catering to various skill levels. This includes 7 easy routes, 17 moderate routes, and 50 difficult routes, offering a wide range of experiences from gentle loops to challenging mountain climbs.

Q: What are the general terrain and elevation characteristics for touring cycling in Sant Celoni?
A: The terrain around Sant Celoni is highly diverse, ranging from mostly paved roads with good surfaces to unpaved segments suitable for gravel cycling. You'll find progressive ascents through winding roads in areas like Montseny Natural Park, with significant climbs such as the 26 km ascent to Turó de l'Home. More moderate terrains with extensive interconnected trails are found in the Montnegre and El Corredor Nature Reserves.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes suitable for a relaxed ride?
A: Yes, for a more relaxed and family-friendly experience, consider routes with a relatively flat profile. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3984068" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llobateres Bird Observatory – Small bird paradise loop from Sant Celoni&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 43.5 km path that offers a gentle ride through natural habitats, ideal for a leisurely outing.

Q: Where can I find challenging touring cycling routes with significant climbs?
A: For challenging rides with significant climbs, the Montseny Natural Park is your destination. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1480257" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Fe del Montseny – Bordoriol Pass loop from Sant Celoni&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 82.8 km trail, feature substantial elevation gains. The climb to Turó de l'Home from Sant Celoni is a notable 26 km ascent with an average gradient of 5%, becoming steeper in the final kilometers.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes that offer a gravel experience?
A: Yes, the region offers excellent gravel cycling opportunities, particularly within the Montnegre and El Corredor Nature Reserves. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974870000/sant-marti-de-montnegre-circular-gravel-through-montnegre-park-and-the-corredor" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Marti de Montnegre - circular gravel through Montnegre Park and the Corredor&lt;/a&gt; route provides a challenging gravel experience through these natural reserves.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I expect to see on touring cycling routes near Sant Celoni?
A: Cycling routes often lead to stunning natural landmarks and viewpoints. In Montseny Natural Park, you can experience high-mountain landscapes and, on clear days, views stretching from Tibidabo to the Mediterranean coast. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1687529" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turó de l'Home (Man's Hill)&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent summit offering panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40348832" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pla de Forn – View of Maresme loop from Sant Celoni&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights a route with coastal views.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions accessible by touring bike in Sant Celoni?
A: Beyond natural beauty, the region is rich in history. You can explore megaliths, dolmens, and Romanesque chapels accessible via the extensive trail networks in Montnegre and El Corredor Nature Reserves. Notable historical sites include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2092377" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montsoriu Castle&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3309501" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hostalric Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1452213" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corredor Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt; is another significant religious building you might encounter.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Sant Celoni?
A: The touring cycling routes around Sant Celoni are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 30 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from dense forests and high-mountain terrain to varied surfaces including both paved and unpaved segments. The options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult climbs, are also highly appreciated.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the Sant Celoni area?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in the Sant Celoni area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974870000/sant-marti-de-montnegre-circular-gravel-through-montnegre-park-and-the-corredor" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Marti de Montnegre - circular gravel&lt;/a&gt; route and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/7790055" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Mosqueroles – Estany d’en Viada loop from Sant Celoni&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a challenging 44.9 km ride.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on touring cycling routes in Sant Celoni?
A: The natural parks surrounding Sant Celoni, particularly Montseny Natural Park and Montnegre and El Corredor Nature Reserves, are rich in diverse flora and fauna. While cycling, you might spot various bird species, especially around areas like the Llobateres Bird Observatory. The dense forests and varied ecosystems provide habitats for a range of wildlife, though specific sightings depend on the season and route.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Sant Celoni?
A: Sant Celoni offers good cycling conditions throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, with lush vegetation in Montseny changing with the seasons. Summer can be warm, especially at lower elevations, while winter rides are possible, though higher mountain routes might be colder. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.
tips:
Objective: rebuild the peace corner of Sant Martí del Montnegre
16/03/2016

A year ago the place suffered a vandal attack and now the Amics de Santa Maria entity, headed by Francesc Vivas from Calella, wants the corner to show off its splendor again.

Francesc Vivas, a lover of the mountain range and who spearheaded the reconstruction of the hermitage of Santa Maria del Montnegre, has explained that with a hammer blow some brats destroyed the "corner of peace of Sant Francesc d'Assís" and they took the image of the saint away, thinking it had more economic value than it really did. Vivas: "There was a pedestal, and on top of it was an image of Saint Francis that had been there for about 50 years. The image was looking up at the sky and praying. The sculpture was about one and a half meters tall and had a patina that appeared to be bronze. Then one night they came with mallets - these very big hammers - and they destroyed the pedestal and with the boom of a crane they took the image, which is now completely lost".

From October 4, 2023 we can once again enjoy the Raco de la Pau
Old church in the middle of beautiful nature.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of Sant Martí de Montnegre
